q: During cold exposure, which thermoregulatory response reduces heat loss in humans?
options: Vasodilation of skin arterioles to radiate more heat
Activation of eccrine sweat glands to increase evaporative cooling
Vasoconstriction of skin blood vessels to reduce blood flow to the body surface
Dilation of pupils to increase heat absorption from the environment
explanation: Vasoconstriction in skin arterioles reduces blood flow near the surface, minimizing heat loss by radiation and conduction to the environment.
